Document:
# Right to Appeal
Anyone under the jurisdiction of the UCMJ has the right to appeal their punishment to their choice of either the Adjutant, CMP, Commander or High Command via fax, who may also choose to designate someone else to handle the appeal in their place. If either person picked is involved in the crimes, they should designate an uninvolved MP/Law Enforcement Officer or warrant/commissioned officer. Whoever is handling the appeal has the final say in the outcome of the appeal.
##Appeal Outcomes & Handling A charge cannot be removed by appeal if the prisoner is found guilty; however, the person who handles the appeal can process the appeal as: - A valid charge, no modification. - Modify the charge to a lower charge. - Reduce the punishment for the charge to the minimum punishment. - Remove a charge for which the prisoner is found innocent.
Appeals must be handled before demotions and NJPs. Appealing prisoners should have their brig timers set and running while the appeal is being processed. Personnel may be placed in holding awaiting their appeal if it had no brig sentence.
Appeals must be held in an enclosed room with a recording of the conversation; the person handling the appeal, the prisoner, the arresting officer and the sentencing officer must all be present, as well as any relevant evidence.
You may only appeal a sentence once. If your appeal is denied, modified, reduced or removed, you may not seek another appeal. Appeals to High Command are reserved for members of Command Staff; otherwise, the Adjutant, CMP or Commander is to handle the appeal.
Appeals must be conducted within 15 minutes. If this timeframe is exceeded, the individual requesting the appeal is to be released from all charges. The sentencing officer or the member of command assigned to the appeal may be charged with Neglect of Duty if it is determined that the delay was intentional and without a permissible reason as defined under appeal delay exemptions.
##Appeal Delays Appeals may only be postponed if: - There are threats on board the ship or installation requiring immediate attention. - Riots or jailbreaks on board the faction ship or installation requiring immediate attention. - The prisoner requires medical treatment. - There is an already-processing appeal. - The prisoner has escaped or is unreachable in person.
